_meta.summary: 20 cards: 6 themed stubs + 14 generic stubs — Byzantines civ deck for MVP-3 playtest (issue #58). NONE of these are OCR-decoded; the Byzantines_Base_EN.pdf OCR pass (#17) confirmed numbers/icons are not extractable at usable confidence. Stat lines and costs are invented to (a) parse against the Card schema, (b) give MVP-3 PlayCard a Byzantines deck that mirrors the English deck's shape (#41), and (c) exercise the broader Card discriminated union (unit/action/tactic/upgrade/technology/event). Themed entries (cataphract, varangian, strategos, greek-fire, basileus, tagmata) use plausible Byzantine military terminology. Generic entries fill in coverage for additional unit types (light cav, archer, naval), actions, tactics, and one each of upgrade/technology/event. The cost-encoding convention from #41 carries forward: every cost is encoded as `wild` until #17 (or a future image-bearing OCR pass) resolves resource-type icons. Refine in #17.

stub_status.note: All 20 entries are STUBS. Every card carries a per-card `_needsConfirmation.stub` block. Refine or replace against the real Byzantine card backs during the #17 OCR pass (or a manual transcription pass — see documentation/ocr/REPORT.md for OCR confidence on the Byzantines source).

_meta.images_status: DEFERRED — no apps/web/public/cards/byzantines/ binaries shipped with this PR. Real card art lands in the same follow-up as English (likely tied to #17 OCR pass with binary PDF access).

stats_confidence.all_fields: LOW — every stat, cost, keyword, class, and subType on every card was invented for MVP-3 playtest variety. Do not balance, playtest as canonical, or reason about gameplay against these numbers. See _meta.stub_status.

do_not: treat any stat / cost / keyword as canonical — every entry is a stub
extend with more fake cards (20 is the MVP-3 target, matching English)
reference these in card-handler logic in @eoe/rules — stubs only

cards.name: Cataphract
cards.flavor: Horse and rider sheathed in scale and lamellar — the iron fist of the Byzantine field army. A wedge of cataphracts could break any line that did not run first.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Themed after the Cataphract heavy cavalry; stat-line intentionally mirrors English Knight (the heavy hitter of that deck) so the two civs have comparable apex units.

cards.name: Varangian Guard
cards.flavor: Northmen sworn to the emperor's person, wielding two-handed axes that no shield wall could weather. Mercenaries by trade, fanatics by oath.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. elite keyword is placeholder vocabulary pending the typed keyword DSL.

cards.name: Strategos
cards.flavor: A general of a theme — civil governor in peace, war-leader in season. His word moves a province.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Strategos modelled as an action (general's rally) rather than a unit so the deck has at least one leadership-flavored action card distinct from a unit.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: original flavor 'ready one exhausted friendly unit and grant +1 movement EoT' has NO mapping in the locked 5-verb Effect DSL (no ready/exhaust verb, and buff-unit-stat.stat does not include movement). Stubbed as {draw, count:1} until a new verb is approved. See .squad/decisions/inbox/sabine-87-mvp6-verb-proposals.md.

cards.name: Greek Fire
cards.flavor: A jealously guarded recipe — naphtha, quicklime, sulphur, and secrets the empire took to its grave. It clung, it burned, and water made it worse.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Phase guessed as deployment (combat-flavored tactic); the rulebook may class Greek Fire as a naval-only effect or a persistent event.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: original flavor 'deal 2 damage to enemy unit adjacent to friendly' had no clean DSL fit (no adjacency primitive, no single-arbitrary-unit target). Remapped to siege-flavored 'damage 2 → opponent-capital' which matches the historical defense-of-Constantinople flavor and is the simplest plausible Effect.

cards.name: Basileus
cards.flavor: Emperor of the Romans, equal to the apostles, autocrat of the world. His face on every coin, his name in every prayer.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: while active, your capital generates +1 gold each Mobilization phase. Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Basileus modelled as a persistent event (the emperor's reign affecting the realm) rather than a unit or upgrade; the rulebook may have a different mechanical home for emperor-themed cards.

cards.name: Tagmata
cards.flavor: The standing regiments of Constantinople — professionals on the imperial payroll, drilled to a standard the themata could only envy.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Slotted between Skoutatoi (basic) and Varangian Guard (apex) on the infantry curve.

cards.name: Skoutatoi
cards.flavor: Heavy infantry of the line, named for the long oval shield. They anchored the centre while the cavalry rolled the flanks.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. shield-wall keyword is placeholder vocabulary.

cards.name: Stratiotai
cards.flavor: Provincial soldier-farmers granted land in exchange for service. Cheap to muster, slow to disband.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Cheap basic-infantry tier of the Byzantine deck.

cards.name: Optimatoi
cards.flavor: Originally a baggage-train and remount corps, the Optimatoi grew into a mounted reserve serving the imperial guard.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. The Byzantines_Base_EN.pdf OCR (#17) spotted this name with possible cavalry/villager hybrid semantics; encoded here as pure cavalry pending confirmation.

cards.name: Toxotai
cards.flavor: Composite-bow archers of the line, equally at home on foot or behind the wall of Skoutatoi.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Pure archer stat-line mirrors English Longbowman's specialist profile.

cards.name: Akritai
cards.flavor: Border lords and their retainers, guarding the frontier marches against Arab raiders. Light, fast, and rarely paid on time.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Hybrid melee/ranged cavalry — skirmish keyword is placeholder vocabulary.

cards.name: Dromon
cards.flavor: Oar-and-sail war galley of the imperial fleet, prow-mounted siphons hurling Greek Fire across the waves.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. naval class and movement.pattern='long' are guesses — confirm whether naval is a first-class unit class in the rules engine, or if naval movement uses a different schema field.

cards.name: Pronoiar
cards.flavor: Landholders who owed mounted service in lieu of taxes. Late-empire stopgap for an army the treasury could no longer pay.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Mid-tier cavalry sitting between Optimatoi/Akritai and Cataphract; mirrors English Mounted Sergeant's role.

cards.name: Themata Muster
cards.flavor: The provinces answer the imperial summons. Where Anatolia and Thrace march, the empire marches.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Mirrors English 'Levy the Fyrd' as the deck's basic draw-action.

cards.name: Imperial Courier
cards.flavor: The cursus publicus — staged horses and signal towers carrying word from the capital to the furthest frontier.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Scry-flavored action gives the deck a tempo / selection tool distinct from raw draw.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: original 'scry 3, keep 1' flavor has NO verb in the locked 5-verb Effect DSL. Stubbed as {draw, count:1} — strictly weaker than the original flavor but preserves the card as a cheap card-advantage option until a 'scry' or 'look-at-top' verb is approved. See .squad/decisions/inbox/sabine-87-mvp6-verb-proposals.md.

cards.name: Fortified Line
cards.flavor: Trench, palisade, and a sky full of arrows. Roman engineering, Greek geometry, infantry doctrine refined for a thousand years.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Mirrors English 'Shield Wall' as a defensive deployment-phase tactic, scaled up for the empire's fortification doctrine.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: 'target infantry unit +2 health EoT' → buff-unit-stat over units-by-class(infantry, own). Same single-vs-set caveat as eng-shield-wall.

cards.name: Naval Blockade
cards.flavor: A chain across the Golden Horn, a fleet across the strait. No grain ship runs to the besieger; no relief lands at the besieged.

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Phase guessed as deployment; the rulebook may class blockade as a persistent event.
_needsConfirmation.effect.mapping: #87: original 'prevent enemy movement next turn' flavor has NO verb in the locked 5-verb Effect DSL (no immobilize / prevent-move primitive). Stubbed as {draw, count:1} until a new verb is approved. See .squad/decisions/inbox/sabine-87-mvp6-verb-proposals.md.

cards.name: Lamellar Armor
cards.flavor: Small iron plates laced into rows — flexible enough to ride in, stiff enough to turn a sword. The standard kit of the empire's professionals.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: attached unit gains armor-1 keyword. Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Mirrors English 'Plate Armor' upgrade; archer/naval classes excluded as a thematic guess.

cards.name: Cataphract Doctrine
cards.flavor: The Strategikon codified the wedge — the order of march, the depth of the line, the moment to charge. Doctrine made the kataphraktoi terrible.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: all friendly cavalry units gain +1 melee. Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. subType 'A' is the schema placeholder per TechnologySubType — real subtype taxonomy TBD.

cards.name: Purple-Born Heir
cards.flavor: Born in the Porphyra chamber, draped in imperial purple from the cradle. The succession is secure; the realm rejoices, briefly.
cards.effect: PLACEHOLDER: while active, the first card you play each turn costs 1 less wild (minimum 0). Effect payload is loose (z.unknown).

_needsConfirmation.stub: STUB — invented for MVP-3 playtest, refine in #17 OCR pass. Second persistent event in the Byzantine deck alongside Basileus; the rulebook caps active events at 3 per player so two stub events leaves headroom.
